## eDNA data standards


::: {style="color:white; padding:15px; border-radius:8px;"}
 **Two major eDNA data standards exist**

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
1. **MiXS** <br>
(Minimum Information about any (x) Sequence) — for raw sequences in NCBI
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
2. **Darwin Core (DwC)**<br>
Community vocabulary for biodiversity data — species, location, date, basis of record
:::
::::

:::

::: {style="text-align:center; color:#065A82; font-weight:bold; font-style:italic; margin-top:10px;"}
OBIS + GBIF + ALA accept genetic data linked to spatial coordinates and time
:::
<br>
🔜**Developing landscape** 

- Rapidly changing field
- For OBIS future directions include the DWC-DP and FAIReDNA terms

::: notes
Raw sequences deposited in NCBI lack coordinates and dates — they cannot answer where or when a species was detected. OBIS, GBIF, and ALA now accept DNA-derived occurrences linked to space and time. DNA data requires special care: it is derived, depends on reference databases, and often produces many unknown sequences. The Darwin Core DNA extension preserves methodological context for future re-analysis.
:::
